_comment: Mirroring/lifecycle hooks are BAKED here (managed-settings = highest precedence, always enforced) so turn-completion is DETERMINISTIC: the Stop hook is the authoritative turn-end signal and StopFailure the authoritative turn-FAILURE signal (both relayed to the cli-agent-py hooks receiver on :8002), NOT herdr idle detection (which surfaced an interactive idle_prompt and wedged one-shot durable/run loops). StopFailure is ALSO registered per-session by _seed_runtime_hooks (belt-and-suspenders: the bake survives a failed per-session seed; double registration is harmless — Stop/StopFailure share an exactly-once (instance_id, turn) completion key). No 'matcher' fields on purpose: omitting the matcher means 'always run' (a 'matcher':'*' entry silently suppressed SessionStart on the ryzen E2E). The two BLOCKING permission hooks (PermissionRequest/PermissionDenied) are deliberately NOT baked here — they return ask/deny and would strand one-shot autoTerminateAfterEndTurn runs that have no human to approve; they are written PER-SESSION for INTERACTIVE sessions only by cli_adapters/claude_code.py::_seed_runtime_hooks.
